methods.description: Process for gathering and anonymizing data from electronic health records and physical notes, including the measurement of clinical severity using QoL scores.

steps.description: Data were gathered via Chameleon electronic health records software and physical notes.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve data from Chameleon EHR and physical notes

steps.description: Data were anonymised for the purposes of analysis.
Clinical severity was measured using two QoL scores completed at each hospital appointment.
steps.searchable_summary: collect QoL scores from hospital appointments

methods.description: Assessment of clinical severity using Rhinoconjunctivitis Quality of Life Questionnaire (RQLQ) and Visual Analogue Score (VAS) for children undergoing AIT.

steps.description: The first was the Rhinoconjunctivitis Quality of Life Questionnaire (RQLQ) score developed by Juniper et al. in 1994.
steps.searchable_summary: use RQLQ to assess QoL in children

steps.description: The second QoL score was the Visual analogue score (VAS) with scores ranging from zero to ten.
steps.searchable_summary: use VAS to assess QoL in children

methods.description: Classification of side-effects experienced by patients into non-anaphylactic, anaphylactic, and asthma categories.

steps.description: Side-effects were divided into three categories: non-anaphylactic, anaphylactic, and asthma.

methods.description: Criteria and process for selecting study population from children receiving SCIT and SLIT for allergies.

steps.description: Data were collected retrospectively from 249 children receiving SCIT and SLIT for pollen, and SLIT for house dust mite allergy.
steps.searchable_summary: collect retrospective data from children receiving SCIT and SLIT

steps.description: Children were considered for AIT if they were sensitised to one or more allergens and their AR remained poorly controlled despite standard therapy.
steps.searchable_summary: select children for AIT based on sensitization and AR control

steps.description: Patients were excluded if their symptoms were well controlled on standard therapy or if they had symptomatic allergy to pet dander and kept a pet at home.
steps.searchable_summary: exclude patients based on symptom control and pet allergy
